article_title: I used ChatGPT to ask better questions during conversations and people actually noticed
core_thesis: The author, a journalist and self-described conversationalist, used ChatGPT to generate alternative, deeper versions of common filler questions (e.g., 'How was your day?') to make everyday conversations more meaningful. By replacing generic questions with more specific, open-ended ones, the author found that friends and family responded with more insightful, emotional, and engaging answers, and people noticed the improvement in the quality of dialogue.
detailed_summary: The article describes an experiment where the author, Elton Jones, used ChatGPT to improve his conversational skills by asking better questions. He started by identifying his own overused filler questions, such as 'How was your day?', 'What do you do for fun?', and 'How are you?'. He then prompted ChatGPT with: 'Give me better versions of everyday questions that make people open up more naturally without sounding intense or fake.' The chatbot provided multiple alternatives for each generic question, designed to elicit deeper responses. For example, instead of 'How was your day?', ChatGPT suggested 'What was the best part of your day so far?' or 'What took up most of your energy today?'. The author tested these questions in conversations with friends, family, and even on a first date, and observed that the responses were more insightful, funny, and emotionally rich. People noticed the shift and appreciated the more meaningful exchanges. The article concludes that ChatGPT can be a practical tool for enhancing communication by providing thoughtful question alternatives that foster deeper connections.

entities.name: Elton Jones
entities.role: Author and AI writer at Tom's Guide who conducted the experiment

entities.name: ChatGPT
entities.role: AI chatbot used to generate better conversation questions

key_data_points: The author used the prompt 'Give me better versions of everyday questions that make people open up more naturally without sounding intense or fake' to get ChatGPT to generate alternative questions (🟢 relevant to your productivity/communication: this prompt can be reused to improve your own conversational skills).

ChatGPT provided multiple alternatives for common filler questions: e.g., instead of 'How was your day?' it suggested 'What was the best part of your day so far?', 'Did anything unexpectedly good happen today?', 'What took up most of your energy today?', and 'What’s one thing you’re glad is over today?' (🟢 practical for student networking or interviews).

Instead of 'What do you do for fun?', ChatGPT suggested 'What’s something you could talk about for way too long?', 'What’s your current obsession?', 'What do you do when you want to disappear for a few hours?', and 'What’s something you always make time for?' (🟢 useful for building rapport in professional or social settings).

The author reported that friends and family gave more insightful, funny, and emotional responses when asked these deeper questions, and they noticed the improvement in conversation quality (🟢 demonstrates real-world effectiveness of AI-assisted communication).

The article also includes a set of first-date icebreaker questions generated by ChatGPT, such as 'What’s something you used to hate but now totally get?' and 'What’s your most shocking hot take?' (🟢 applicable for social situations and building connections).
methodology_steps: Identify your own overused filler questions (e.g., 'How was your day?', 'What do you do for fun?', 'How are you?').
Open ChatGPT and use the prompt: 'Give me better versions of everyday questions that make people open up more naturally without sounding intense or fake.'
Review the alternative questions provided by ChatGPT for each filler question.
Incorporate these alternative questions into your everyday conversations with friends, family, coworkers, or acquaintances.
Observe the responses and note any increase in depth, emotion, or engagement.
Optionally, ask ChatGPT for specific sets of questions for particular contexts (e.g., first dates) using a similar prompt.
conclusions: Using ChatGPT to generate deeper, more specific questions can significantly improve the quality of everyday conversations, leading to more meaningful exchanges and stronger connections. The author's friends and family noticed the change and responded positively.

The experiment shows that AI tools like ChatGPT can be effectively used for soft skill development, such as communication and social interaction, not just technical or productivity tasks. This is particularly valuable for students and professionals looking to enhance their interpersonal skills.

The key is to replace generic, closed-ended questions with open-ended, curiosity-driven ones that invite storytelling and emotional sharing. The article provides a practical, repeatable method for anyone to improve their conversational abilities.
personal_relevance_summary: As a student and ambitious individual, this article offers a practical, low-effort method to improve your communication skills using ChatGPT. You can directly apply the provided prompts and question alternatives to enhance networking, group projects, interviews, and social interactions. The technique is especially useful for building rapport and making conversations more engaging, which can benefit both academic and professional settings. Additionally, the article demonstrates a creative use of AI beyond technical tasks, aligning with your interest in leveraging technology for personal growth.
extra_advice: Consider creating a personal 'conversation starter' cheat sheet using ChatGPT-generated questions tailored to different contexts (e.g., networking events, study groups, first dates). You could also experiment with asking ChatGPT to role-play a conversation partner to practice these questions in a safe environment before using them in real life.
